The '''Nharwaak''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that, along with the [[Habeen]], populated the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Mylok IV]] in the [[Mylok system/Legends|Mylok system]].

The Nharwaak were heavily dependent on [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]] to form the backbone of their [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. They also used older [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], such as the [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]].

==Hyperdrive innovation==
Together, [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] from these two species worked together to develop a radical new [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] design that was compact and cost-effective enough to be feasibly incorporated into mass-produced TIE [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] (as opposed to the prototype [[TIE Advanced x1/Legends|TIE Advanced x1]] design flown by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] during the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]). Starfighter hyperdrives had been a thorny problem for [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] since well before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Indeed, before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] even [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] starfighters were typically lacking internal hyperdrives and used a [[Hyperspace transport ring/Legends|Hyperdrive docking ring]] for interstellar travel.

As such, the joint Habeen/Nharwaak hyperdrive technology was a critical technological development that would have far-reaching implications for both the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the opposing force of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].

In supplying customers, whether through fear or loyalty, the Habeen chose to serve the Empire, in the form of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin|Zaarin]]. The Nharwaak, on the other hand, perhaps out of idealism, decided to provide the hyperdrive to the Rebellion.

===Convoy attack===
Shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], when the Habeen began the transfer of the new technology to Zaarin's forces aboard the [[Nebulon-B2 frigate]] ''[[Shamus]]'', the Nharwaak attacked the rendezvous using a pair of CR90 corvettes, the [[Demetrius Zaarin|''Charger 1'']] and ''Charger 2'', and a number of Y-wings and [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]]. This attack was unsuccessful and the convoy survived, in part due to the piloting skills of future [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]], [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]], who would play an important part in thwarting the further aims of the Nharwaak.

===Imperial counterstrike===
The Imperial retaliation was swift. With the discovery of a secret Nharwaak base hidden among a cache of [[Xizor cargo container]] lightly defended by Y-wings, Admiral Zaarin was able to mount a [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bomber]] attack from the Modified Frigate ''Shamus''. The destruction of the workshop was a setback that severely crippled the Nharwaak scientists' ability to conduct research and development. The attack also destroyed a number of CR90 corvettes, ''[[Petor]]'' group and [[Shotgun (CR90 corvette)|''Shotgun'']] group, that were carrying missiles and torpedoes, further denting the Nharwaak's offensive capabilities.

===Attack on the Habeen===
Following the destruction of the hidden base, the Nharwaak set about retaliating by annihilating a Habeen tech center near Mylok IV. Unable to send the ''Shamus'' in time, Zaarin dispatched the [[CR92a Assassin-class corvette|''Assassin''-class corvette]] [[Phoenix (Assassin-class)|''Phoenix'']] with TIE Bombers and [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptors]] to defend the Habeen installation. Although intent on inflicting heavy damage, the Nharwaak were eventually driven off by the heroic efforts of Maarek Stele, while the Habeen work crew and station supplies were successfully evacuated.

===Diplomatic incident===
When the Habeen decided to normalize diplomatic relations with the Empire, the Nharwaak tried to sabotage Zaarin's negotiations aboard the ''Shamus'' by sending Y-wings and the corvettes of ''[[Phazer]]'' group to attack the Habeen [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B frigate]] ''[[Lendova]]''. Again, Maarek Stele's genius in a TIE Interceptor saved the day by almost single-handedly turning back the Nharwaak fighters. The diplomatic meeting was a complete success, and the Habeen agreed to turn over the new hyperdrive technology to the Empire.

===Rebel arms deal===
In response to the Habeen's deal, the Nharwaak initiated a deal with the Rebels to turn over the same technology. A meeting was arranged between the trio of Nharwaak CR90 corvettes, ''[[Glazer]]'', ''[[Coffey]]'', and [[Janson (CR90 corvette)|''Janson'']], and the Rebel Nebulon-B Frigate [[Xerxes (EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate)|''Xerxes'']]. Zaarin cunningly allowed this meeting to begin, before interrupting it with a deadly ambush of [[Alpha-class Xg-1 Star Wing/Legends|Assault Gunboats]] and TIE Bombers from the ''Shamus''. The Rebel frigate was obliterated, and with it, the Nharwaak's hopes of aiding the Rebellion.
